title: Database connection strings with credentials should not be disclosed
description: Database connection strings that include embedded usernames and passwords in the URI expose database credentials in a single convenient format for attackers. These strings provide direct access to databases containing application data, user information, and business-critical records.
recommendation: Remove credentials from database connection strings in source code. Use environment variables for connection strings, or construct them at runtime from separately managed credentials. Consider using IAM-based authentication where available.
impact: Direct unauthorized access to databases containing sensitive user data, business records, and application secrets.

checks.message: PostgreSQL connection string with embedded credentials detected. This connection string exposes a database username and password granting direct access to your PostgreSQL database. Attackers can use this to read, modify, or delete all accessible data, execute arbitrary SQL, dump tables containing user credentials or PII, and potentially escalate privileges. Remove credentials from the connection string and use environment variables or IAM authentication.

MySQL connection string with embedded credentials detected. This connection string exposes MySQL database credentials providing direct access to query and modify all databases the user can access. Exposed MySQL credentials can lead to data theft, database manipulation, and credential pivoting to other systems. Rotate credentials and use environment variables.

MongoDB connection string with embedded credentials detected. This connection string exposes MongoDB credentials providing access to NoSQL databases that may contain user records, application data, and business information. MongoDB URIs with credentials allow full collection access based on role permissions. Revoke credentials and use environment variables or MongoDB Atlas secret manager integration.

Redis connection string with embedded password detected. Redis instances often store session data, cached credentials, and application secrets. Exposing Redis connection credentials allows reading and writing all data in the Redis instance, potentially accessing session tokens, cached API keys, and other sensitive application data. Rotate the Redis password and use environment variables.

JDBC connection string with embedded credentials detected. JDBC connection strings expose database credentials for Java applications. These can provide access to any JDBC-compatible database including Oracle, SQL Server, MySQL, and PostgreSQL. Remove credentials from JDBC URLs and use application server credential stores or environment variables.

AMQP/RabbitMQ connection string with embedded credentials detected. Message broker credentials provide access to queues and exchanges that may contain sensitive application events, financial transactions, and inter-service messages. Exposing AMQP credentials can lead to message interception, injection of malicious messages, and service disruption. Rotate credentials and use environment variables.
